reforms.description: The Israeli government announced an increase in the Chief Scientist Fund budget to enhance R&D investments across various sectors, including biotechnology and peripheral regions. Additionally, the Shochat Committee proposed a substantial hike in state-budget transfers to universities and colleges, along with structural reforms to raise non-state revenues. The government also supports business incubators by providing grants and assistance to entrepreneurs for R&D projects, crucial for fostering innovation in high-tech sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding and support for R&D activities are expected to enhance innovation and productivity in high-tech sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been put into practice, with funding and support mechanisms actively functioning.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms involve significant budget increases and structural changes, but do not represent a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: R&D investments: increase in Chief Scientist Fund budget (NIS0.35billion), Bio-Technology Fund (NIS0.25billion), and Periphery R&D Fund (NIS0.05billion).

reforms.description: The NewHorizon programme was launched to reform primary and lower-secondary education by increasing teachers' workweek and improving compensation, aiming to enhance teaching quality and student performance.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving education quality is expected to enhance the skills of future researchers and innovators, contributing to long-term economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been actively implemented since its launch, with changes in teacher work conditions and compensation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant structural change in the education system aimed at improving teaching quality.
reforms.source_quote: The NewHorizon programme was made compulsory for all primary and lower secondary schools for the first time in the 2009-10 school year.

reforms.description: Israel's business support programmes include innovation policies aimed at fostering high-technology and R&D activities, particularly in poorer regions of the country.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Supporting high-tech and R&D activities is expected to stimulate economic growth and innovation in underdeveloped regions.
reforms.status_evidence: The innovation policies have been actively implemented to support high-tech sectors.
reforms.importance_rationale: The policies represent a significant effort to enhance innovation but do not constitute a major overhaul of existing systems.
reforms.source_quote: Israel’s business support programmes can be divided into three categories: investment incentives that are primarily aimed at attracting businesses to poorer regions; innovation policies; and, programmes for small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs).

reforms.description: Israel's government has implemented a competitive grant system to promote R&D activities, requiring firms to repay funding if their projects yield commercial returns, effectively functioning as high-risk loans.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The competitive grant system incentivizes private sector R&D investment, fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The competitive grant system is currently in operation, with firms actively participating.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of a competitive grant system represents a significant change in how R&D funding is allocated.
reforms.source_quote: At the core of innovation policy is a 'grant' system, which bears similarities with mechanisms elsewhere, except for an obligation to pay back the funding if projects reap commercial returns.
